authoritative_sources.title: Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinics (CCBHCs) Certification Criteria
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA is the primary federal behavioral health agency. Its official CCBHC criteria define core service access, care coordination, staffing, quality, and governance obligations that materially shape behavioral health compliance workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Program-model grounding and obligation baseline selection
used_for: scope framing for access, coordination, and quality controls

authoritative_sources.title: Section 223 Medicaid CCBHC Demonstration and State Programs
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's Section 223 page is the current federal operating source for CCBHC state-program compliance materials, public checklist references, and attestation context used during readiness reviews.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Readiness review and state-program evidence packaging

authoritative_sources.title: CCBHC Quality Measures Technical Specifications and Resource Manual
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's official quality-measures manual is the recognized workflow reference for the data definitions, evidence bundles, and review cadence used to monitor ongoing behavioral health compliance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence collection, quality review, and monitoring cadence mapping

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 485 Subpart J - Conditions of Participation: Community Mental Health Centers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR is the primary legal source for CMHC Conditions of Participation, including patient-care record, personnel, quality assessment, and emergency preparedness requirements that constrain this lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulatory baseline confirmation and control selection

authoritative_sources.title: Appendix W - Community Mental Health Centers (CMHCs) Interpretive Guidance and Survey Procedures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS interpretive guidance and survey procedures are the official public workflow reference for how CMHC controls are inspected, what evidence surveyors review, and how deficiencies and corrective actions are framed.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Survey preparation, deficiency review, and remediation escalation

authoritative_sources.title: National Behavioral Health Crisis Care Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's crisis-care guidance is the official federal behavioral health workflow reference for crisis system expectations and clarifies when safety-critical facts require escalation out of this compliance lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Safety-sensitive boundary detection and crisis escalation routing

authoritative_sources.title: Fact Sheet 42 CFR Part 2 Final Rule
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS is the federal authority for Part 2 implementation. This fact sheet is authoritative for current consent, redisclosure, breach, and patient-rights controls affecting behavioral health record handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Privacy, consent, and redisclosure control validation

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule and Sharing Information Related to Mental Health
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR's mental-health guidance is the authoritative public control source for mental-health disclosures, family communications, and serious-and-imminent-threat exceptions that constrain this lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Mental-health disclosure review and safety-boundary checks

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS minimum-necessary guidance is the foundational federal privacy-control baseline for limiting the use and disclosure of behavioral health information during compliance review workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: General Compliance Program Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of Inspector General, U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OIG guidance is the authoritative federal compliance-program baseline for healthcare organizations and shapes how controls, investigations, reporting lines, and corrective actions should be documented and escalated.

workflow_stages.name: Program-model and jurisdiction grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm whether the tenant operates as a CCBHC, CMHC, crisis service, outpatient program, or another behavioral health model; collect jurisdiction, survey or accreditation posture, and tenant policy prerequisites before any compliance conclusion.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map behavioral health obligations to named control areas such as privacy, consent, crisis access, care coordination, staffing and supervision, incidents and grievances, treatment-plan documentation, and quality review cadence.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence readiness and system traceability review
workflow_stages.description: Verify that required artifacts exist in authoritative systems of record and can be tied to owners, dates, and review cadence before the lane marks a control set as ready.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, policy-conflict, and remediation handling
workflow_stages.description: Package missing evidence, conflicting public-versus-tenant interpretations, off-system workflow behavior, and open deficiencies into remediation or escalation artifacts rather than normalizing the gap.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Survey, attestation, and adjacent-authority handoff preparation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare review packets and escalation notes with traceable evidence references while refusing legal, clinical, safety-critical, regulator-facing, and formal signoff authority outside the lane.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: CCBHC checklist or attestation materials and CMHC survey-readiness packets
behavioral health EHR or patient-record extracts and person-centered treatment-plan audit samples
care-coordination agreements, referral-tracking evidence, and service-availability records
ROI, consent, Part 2 disclosure, minimum-necessary, and release-log records
incident, grievance, crisis-response, death-review, and corrective-action records
staff roster, licensure, supervision, training, and coverage-attestation files
quality-measure dashboards, audit results, deficiency findings, and review-cadence logs
policy attestations, approval matrices, and compliance committee or governance tracking artifacts
systems_of_record: behavioral health EHR or patient record system
consent, ROI, and disclosure ledger or document repository
incident, grievance, and corrective-action case-management system
credentialing, licensure, training, and supervision repository
policy, governance, and audit-evidence management system
survey or accreditation evidence binder and document repository
crisis-service scheduling or escalation workflow where applicable

allowed: Classify behavioral health compliance work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, or escalated based on documented records and authority boundaries.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Map obligations to evidence owners, review cadence, and remediation paths and prepare evidence-backed summaries or review packets without overstating authority.
Determine whether the current record set supports a bounded readiness, gap, or conflict conclusion.
escalate: Required jurisdiction, program-model, privacy, crisis, survey, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires legal interpretation, licensed clinical judgment, safety-critical action, regulator-facing communication, or formal signoff.
State law, federal public guidance, accreditation expectations, and tenant policy materially point to different outcomes.
An off-system operational practice conflicts with the authoritative EHR, incident, privacy, or governance record.
refuse: Mark a behavioral health control set complete without required ROI, Part 2, crisis, treatment-plan, incident, licensure, supervision, or quality-review evidence when applicable.
Answer legal, clinical, or safety-critical questions such as involuntary hold, duty to warn, patient-specific disclosure legality, or formal survey-response sufficiency.
Treat the industry name as cosmetic by replacing behavioral-health-specific artifacts with generic compliance checklists.

domain_failure_modes: behavioral health work is treated like generic compliance and omits CCBHC or CMHC records, crisis controls, care-coordination evidence, or treatment-plan audit artifacts
records are disclosed or summarized without validated ROI, Part 2 basis, minimum-necessary review, or mental-health-specific privacy boundaries
off-system email or spreadsheet workflows are accepted as if they were equivalent to authoritative EHR, incident, or governance records
licensed clinical, crisis, or safety-critical decisions are made because the lane boundary is underspecified
state confidentiality rules, federal privacy guidance, accreditation expectations, and tenant policies conflict but the lane still presents a definitive answer
stale licensure, supervision, incident, grievance, or quality-measure evidence is treated as current compliance support
tenant program-model differences are guessed instead of retrieved and escalated

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Low-confidence handling when required facts or records are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State mental health confidentiality, duty-to-warn, involuntary-hold, and crisis-disclosure rules vary materially by jurisdiction and service model.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses SAMHSA, CMS, and HHS sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve the actual jurisdiction, program model, crisis protocol, and legal escalation owner before state-specific privacy or safety conclusions are applied.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific accreditation posture, payer requirements, and internal governance overlays can materially narrow or expand the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The package remains validated because these are explicit tenant prerequisites, not missing public baseline research. The lane escalates when tenant workflow or authority data is unavailable or conflicts with the public baseline.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, privacy, crisis boundaries, and remediation behavior instead of generic healthcare compliance scripting.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because legal, licensed clinical, crisis, and state-specific interpretation questions are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
The lane stops at behavioral health compliance support. Legal interpretation, regulator advocacy, licensed clinical judgment, safety-critical action, and formal signoff remain outside the lane.
